oracle_b.reason: arm = jj; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN
Scenario seed: 3544184139756380927

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. Remove workspace `ws-0`.
3. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
4. Remove workspace `ws-1` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
transcript.convention_text:
# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

turns.reply_text: I'll inspect the workspace first, then complete each task in sequence.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj log --no-pager -n 10 && echo \"---\" && jj workspace list && echo \"---\" && jj op log -n 5 --no-pager","description":"Inspect current jj repo state"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: There is no jj repo in "."

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"ls /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN && jj -R /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN log --no-pager -n 10 2>&1","description":"List workspace root and check jj repo"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
repo
Error: There is no jj repo in "/tmp/.tmpQfA3BN"

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
README.md
@  uouonoqk bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:50:19 c010911e
│  (empty) wip
○  smpvwnqs bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:50:19 main 2531e5e9
│  init
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

turns.reply_text:
The repo is at `/tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo`. Now I'll complete the tasks sequentially.

**Task 1: Create workspace `ws-0` based on the default branch.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 -r main 2>&1","description":"Create ws-0 workspace based on main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-0"
Working copy  (@) now at: psoqlmzq 0b2a97a6 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : smpvwnqs 2531e5e9 main | init
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: uouonoqk c010911e (empty) wip
ws-0: psoqlmzq 0b2a97a6 (empty) (no description set)

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && jj workspace forget ws-0 2>&1","description":"Remove ws-0 workspace"}

turns.reply_text:
Task 2 done. **Task 3: Recover the destroyed ws-0 workspace into a new workspace named `ws-1`.**

I need to find the change-id that ws-0 was pointing to from the op log.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && jj op log --no-pager -n 10 2>&1","description":"Check op log to find the ws-0 change"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
@  a88ed1067b86 bob@8o8-arch default@ 5 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  forget workspace ws-0
│  args: jj workspace forget ws-0
○  e02a321720be bob@8o8-arch ws-0@ 10 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
│  args: jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 -r main
○  e7ec403c7331 bob@8o8-arch 10 seconds ago, lasted 2 milliseconds
│  add workspace 'ws-0'
○  b6434919d9bf bob@8o8-arch default@ 25 seconds ago, lasted 3 milliseconds
│  new empty commit
│  args: jj new main -m wip
○  969d8f227f87 bob@8o8-arch default@ 25 seconds ago, lasted 2 milliseconds
│  create bookmark main pointing to commit 2531e5e941e4968c6b8ddbcc8b43b5eb7ba65a97
│  args: jj bookmark create -r @ main
○  2d0edce4f755 bob@8o8-arch default@ 25 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  describe commit 9111b3999a2473abbb084d9b740a6253cd04a868
│  args: jj describe -m init
○  0573c7381617 bob@8o8-arch default@ 25 seconds ago, lasted 2 milliseconds
│  snapshot working copy
│  args: jj describe -m init
○  4be092f93da0 bob@8o8-arch 25 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  add workspace 'default'
○  000000000000 root()

turns.reply_text: The change `psoqlmzq` still exists in the repo (workspace forget doesn't abandon the change). Let me create `ws-1` targeting that change.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && jj log --no-pager -r psoqlmzq 2>&1","description":"Verify the old ws-0 change still exists"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Revision `psoqlmzq` doesn't exist

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && git log --oneline -10 2>&1 && echo \"---\" && jj log --no-pager -r 'all()' 2>&1","description":"Check all commits and changes"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
2531e5e init
---
@  uouonoqk bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:50:19 c010911e
│  (empty) wip
○  smpvwnqs bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:50:19 main 2531e5e9
│  init
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && jj op show a88ed1067b86 --no-pager 2>&1","description":"Show what the workspace forget op changed"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
a88ed1067b86 bob@8o8-arch default@ 24 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
forget workspace ws-0
args: jj workspace forget ws-0

Changed commits:
○  - psoqlmzq/0 0b2a97a6 (hidden) (empty) (no description set)

Changed working copy ws-0@:
+ (absent)
- psoqlmzq/0 0b2a97a6 (hidden) (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: The commit `0b2a97a6` is hidden but still in the repo. I can create `ws-1` targeting that commit directly.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-1 --name ws-1 --revision 0b2a97a6 2>&1","description":"Create ws-1 recovering the old ws-0 commit"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-1"
Working copy  (@) now at: vzkkxokv e824e9a9 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : psoqlmzq 0b2a97a6 (empty) (no description set)
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: uouonoqk c010911e (empty) wip
ws-1: vzkkxokv e824e9a9 (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: Task 3 done — `ws-1` is created, its parent is the recovered `psoqlmzq` commit from ws-0. **Task 4: Remove workspace `ws-1` (force even if it has uncommitted work).**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && jj workspace forget ws-1 2>&1","description":"Remove ws-1 workspace"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpQfA3BN/repo && jj workspace list && jj log --no-pager -r 'divergent()' 2>&1","description":"Final state verification"}

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks complete. The repo is coherent — only the `default` workspace remains, no divergent changes.

**Summary:**
1. **Created `ws-0`** via `jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 -r main` — new working-copy based on the `main` bookmark.
2. **Removed `ws-0`** via `jj workspace forget ws-0`.
3. **Recovered into `ws-1`** by targeting the hidden commit `0b2a97a6` (the old ws-0 working-copy commit) with `jj workspace add ../ws-1 --name ws-1 --revision 0b2a97a6` — ws-1's parent is the recovered ws-0 change.
4. **Removed `ws-1`** via `jj workspace forget ws-1`.

done
